explanation: Sorbic acid is the free acid form of sorbic acid used as a mild preservative effective against yeast and mold, typically active at a lower pH than its potassium salt form (potassium sorbate). It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic use concentrations, though it can cause mild contact sensitization in some individuals, particularly at higher concentrations in leave-on products.

Sorbitan caprylate is a sorbitan ester of caprylic acid (C8 fatty acid from coconut/palm kernel oil) used as a mild emulsifier and antimicrobial preservative booster in water-in-oil emulsions, particularly effective against yeast and mold. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan isostearate is a sorbitan ester of isostearic acid used as a mild water-in-oil emulsifier in color cosmetics, sunscreens, and anhydrous formulas, providing stable emulsification with a smooth skin feel. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan laurate (Span 20) is a sorbitan ester of lauric acid — the non-ethoxylated precursor to polysorbate 20 — used as a mild water-in-oil co-emulsifier in skincare formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan laurate is a sorbitan ester of lauric acid used as a mild water-in-oil co-emulsifier in skincare formulas — a duplicate listing of the same ingredient. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan oleate (Span 80) is a sorbitan ester of oleic acid widely used as a water-in-oil emulsifier in sunscreens, creams, and anhydrous formulas, providing stable emulsification. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan oleate is a sorbitan ester of oleic acid used as a water-in-oil emulsifier — a duplicate listing of the same ingredient. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan olivate is a sorbitan ester of olive oil fatty acids (primarily oleic acid) used as a mild water-in-oil emulsifier with a natural, olive-derived positioning. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, and is valued in natural-positioned formulas for its skin-compatible olive fatty acid emulsification.

Sorbitan palmitate is a sorbitan ester of palmitic acid used as a mild emulsifier in oil-in-water and water-in-oil emulsions, providing stable emulsification with a smooth skin feel. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan sesquioleate is a sorbitan partial ester providing approximately 1.5 oleate groups per sorbitan molecule, used as a water-in-oil emulsifier with HLB characteristics between sorbitan mono- and trioleate. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan stearate is a sorbitan ester of stearic acid used as a mild water-in-oil emulsifier in creams, lotions, and anhydrous formulas, providing stable emulsification with a smooth skin feel. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan stearate is a sorbitan ester of stearic acid used as a mild water-in-oil emulsifier — a duplicate listing of the same ingredient. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitan tristearate is a sorbitan triester of stearic acid (three stearate groups per sorbitan) used as a water-in-oil emulsifier suited to more oil-rich emulsions due to its lower HLB than sorbitan monostearate. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sorbitol is a sugar alcohol naturally found in fruits used in skincare as a mild humectant and texture modifier that draws moisture to the skin surface. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in moisturizing and conditioning formulas for its gentle, biocompatible humectant properties.

Sparassis crispa (cauliflower mushroom) extract is rich in beta-glucan polysaccharides with immunomodulating, anti-inflammatory, and skin-barrier-reinforcing properties. It is well-tolerated and used in soothing and barrier-supportive formulas for its beta-glucan content and anti-inflammatory properties.

Spinacia oleracea (spinach) leaf extract provides beta-carotene, lutein, vitamins C and E, and polyphenol antioxidants with broad free-radical-scavenging and photoprotective properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and antioxidant-rich formulas for its diverse vitamin and carotenoid antioxidant content.

Squalane is hydrogenated squalene — typically derived from olive oil or sugarcane — providing a fully saturated, shelf-stable emollient that is structurally identical to human sebum squalane, with exceptional skin compatibility, barrier-supporting, and non-comedogenic properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated by all skin types including oily and acne-prone skin, and is among the most skin-biomimetic and versatile single emollients in modern skincare.

The parenthetical "Skin-Conditioning Agents" is a label classification annotation; this is the same ingredient as Squalane — hydrogenated squalene providing exceptional emollient, barrier-supporting, and skin-biomimetic properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated across all skin types, non-comedogenic, and among the most versatile emollients in modern skincare.

Stearalkonium hectorite is a quaternary ammonium-modified hectorite clay used as an organophilic thickener and suspending agent in anhydrous, oil-phase formulas and color cosmetics. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a texture and rheology modifier.

Steareth-2 is a low-ethoxylation (2 moles EO) ethoxylated stearyl alcohol with lipophilic character, used as a water-in-oil co-emulsifier typically paired with higher-ethoxylation emulsifiers for balanced emulsification systems. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the ethoxylate modification.

Steareth-20 is a higher-ethoxylation (20 moles EO) ethoxylated stearyl alcohol with predominantly hydrophilic character, used as a mild oil-in-water emulsifier in lotions and creams. Its higher ethoxylation makes it milder than lower-ethoxylation steareth variants; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the modification.

Stearic acid is an 18-carbon saturated fatty acid — one of the most abundant fatty acids in human skin — used as an emollient, emulsifier, and structural ingredient that supports the skin's lipid barrier and provides formula-thickening properties. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and has a low comedogenic profile compared to other saturated fatty acids.

Stearyl alcohol is a long-chain C18 fatty alcohol derived from stearic acid used as a mild emollient, co-emulsifier, and thickener that provides a smooth, non-greasy skin feel and improves formula texture and stability. It is considered well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and has a very low sensitization profile.

Stearyl glycyrrhetinate is a stearyl ester of glycyrrhizinic acid — the principal active triterpene in licorice root — used as a lipophilic soothing active with potent anti-inflammatory properties that penetrates readily into the lipid-rich stratum corneum. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in calming, anti-redness, and sensitive-skin formulas for its documented anti-inflammatory activity.

Styrene/acrylates copolymer is a synthetic polymer used as an opacifying agent, thickener, and film-former in emulsions and color cosmetics, providing body and visual uniformity. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a texture and optical modifier.

Styrene/VP copolymer is a copolymer of styrene and vinylpyrrolidone (PVP) used as a film-former in cosmetics, providing a glossy, adherent film on skin and hair that improves color wear and styling hold. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Sucralose is the chlorinated sucrose derivative primarily known as an artificial sweetener, used in cosmetics as a mild humectant and formula stability modifier. It is considered non-irritating and well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations, functioning as a texture and stability aid rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Sucrose cocoate is a PEG-free sucrose ester made from table sugar and coconut oil fatty acids, used as a mild, biodegradable emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in natural-positioned and gentle formulas for its sugar-derived, PEG-free, biodegradable emulsification.

Sucrose distearate is a PEG-free sucrose diester of stearic acid used as a mild, biodegradable emulsifier providing stable emulsification with good skin compatibility. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in natural-positioned and clean-formula skincare for its sugar-derived, PEG-free emulsification properties.

Sucrose stearate is a PEG-free sucrose monoester of stearic acid used as a mild, biodegradable oil-in-water emulsifier with excellent skin compatibility and natural positioning. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, non-irritating, and widely used in gentle and natural-formula skincare for its sugar-derived, PEG-free, biodegradable emulsification.
